subject: Ecology
title: Invasive Species in Wetlands
description: A short lesson to help you apply Invasive Species in Wetlands.

hook.text: Purple loosestrife turns a diverse marsh into a single-species monoculture—beautiful but deadly.

buildup.text: Invasive plants outcompete natives, reducing food and habitat for wetland animals.

discovery.text: Invasive carp in North American rivers eat plankton so efficiently they starve native fish.

twist.text: Biological control—introducing insects that eat only the invasive plant—has tamed loosestrife.

climax.text: Early detection and rapid response are the most cost-effective strategies against wetland invaders.

punchline.text: The prettiest invader is still an invader.

quiz.question: How was purple loosestrife controlled in North American wetlands?
options: By introducing host-specific insects that eat it
